rationale: Both candidates cover the core correctness confirmation and note weaknesses in the new test's coverage of the outer AutoWeightsLoader mapping. But Y grounds every claim in actual upstream vLLM source lines (utils.py, weight_utils.py, fp8.py) and correctly flags that hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still called the removed API and was left out of scope — precisely the latent gap that later became issue #4891. X instead investigated that same file and reached the wrong conclusion ('has only a comment, no broken code'), and padded its assumptions with unverifiable/likely-fabricated specifics (a cited vLLM PR #43167, a version number) that hurt precision. Both are reasonably actionable with file/line citations, but Y's are more concrete and correct.
